  • The Kiritsuke Gyuto, also known as the K-tip gyuto, possesses a reverse tanto tip, which makes it less suitable for 'rock chopping' ingredients. However, its slightly flat profile makes it highly effective as a slicer.
  • The 10Cr15CoMoV steel meets the standards of a chef-friendly steel. With a respectable hardness achieved through heat treatment, it offers the added advantage of being stainless steel, reducing the hassle of daily maintenance.
  • A leather sheath is included with this product, helping you prevent the knife from damaging itself.

Good for

Meat, Fish, Vegetables, Fruit, Herbs

Name: LeeKnives Japanese Wa Gyuto with Rosewood Handle
Short description:
Key Features

  • The Japanese all-purpose Gyuto boasts a versatile 200 mm length, allowing for efficient slicing, dicing, and chopping of various ingredients. It can seamlessly substitute ordinary chef knives, accommodating a range of culinary tasks.
  • Crafted with a traditional eight-faceted Japanese wa-handle, this timeless piece features the exquisite use of rosewood as its material, adding elegance and sophistication to its design.

Good for

Meat, Fish, Vegetables, Fruit, Herbs
